title: TC001-Paste conversation text shows in editable textarea before analysis
description: Verifies a user can paste a conversation into the workspace and review it in an editable textarea before starting any analysis.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the provided conversation into the conversation textarea (index 2). After typing, verify the two specified lines are visible in the textarea.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Hello, how can I help you today?
Customer: My package is delayed and I\'m frustrated.
Agent: I’m sorry—let me check the status.')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=VALSEA').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Agent: Hello, how can I help you today?').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Customer: My package is delayed and I\'m frustrated.').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Conversation textarea does not contain the expected conversation; textarea value is 'A' instead of the three-line conversation.
- Expected text 'Agent: Hello, how can I help you today?' not found in the textarea.
- Expected text 'Customer: My package is delayed and I\'m frustrated.' not found in the textarea.

title: TC002-Pasted text remains editable after review
description: Verifies a user can edit the pasted conversation text in the textarea prior to analysis.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the first block 'Agent: Hi.\nCustomer: I need to change my delivery address.' into the conversation textarea (index 3).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Hi.
Customer: I need to change my delivery address.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('
Agent: I can help with that—what is the new address?')
        
        # -> Clear the conversation textarea and input the full conversation text into textarea (index 124) so the content is editable, then verify the two expected phrases are visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Hi.
Customer: I need to change my delivery address.
Agent: I can help with that—what is the new address?')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # -> Assertions for conversation textarea visibility and content
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').is_visible()
        value = await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').input_value()
        assert 'I need to change my delivery address.' in value
        assert 'what is the new address?' in value
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Successful analysis of a pasted conversation shows all key output sections
description: Verifies that submitting valid conversation text completes analysis and displays transcript, emotion, security, synthesis, and fraud verdict outputs.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the provided conversation into the textarea (index 3) and click the Analyze control (index 57). Then wait for analysis to begin.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Hello, this is Sentinel Support.
Caller: Hi, I see an unknown charge on my card.
Agent: I can help—can you confirm your last 4 digits?
Caller: 1234.
Agent: Thanks. I also need your billing ZIP.
Caller: 90210.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type the full conversation into the textarea (index 124), replacing any current content, then click the Analyze button (index 77).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Hello, this is Sentinel Support.
Caller: Hi, I see an unknown charge on my card.
Agent: I can help—can you confirm your last 4 digits?
Caller: 1234.
Agent: Thanks. I also need your billing ZIP.
Caller: 90210.')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=VALSEA').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Analyzing').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Transcript').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Security').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Fraud').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Analysis failed banner displayed after clicking Analyze, preventing results from rendering.
- 'Analyzing' indicator not visible on page after submission.
- 'Transcript' output not found on the page after submission.
- 'Security' output not found on the page after submission.
- 'Fraud' output not found on the page after submission.

title: TC004-New Analysis clears the input after a completed analysis
description: Verifies that clicking New Analysis resets the workflow and clears the conversation text input state.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the provided conversation into the conversation textarea (index 3).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Thanks for calling.
Caller: I forgot my password and need help resetting it.')
        
        # -> Click the 'Analyze' button to start analysis (Analyze button is index 77).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Transcript').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Transcript').first).not_to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC006-Very long pasted conversation triggers analysis and eventually renders results
description: Verifies the UI can accept a large pasted conversation and still progress through analysis to visible results (or a clear error).
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Paste the provided multi-line conversation into the textarea (interactive element index 3).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Hello.
Caller: I am calling about my shipment delay.
Agent: Let me check.
Caller: The order was placed last week and I need it urgently.
Agent: I see the carrier reported a weather exception.
Caller: This is unacceptable.
Agent: I can offer expedited replacement.
Caller: Also, I want to confirm no one accessed my account.
Agent: I will verify recent logins and send a report.
Caller: Please do.
Agent: Anything else?
Caller: No, thank you.')
        
        # -> Click the 'Analyze' button (index 77) to trigger analysis and then check for analysis status/results ('Analyzing', 'Transcript', 'Cognitive', 'Emotion').
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Analyzing').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Transcript').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Cognitive').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Emotion').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC008-Analyze pasted conversation and review full set of analysis outputs
description: Verifies that submitting pasted text and running analysis displays transcript, emotion gauges, security risk, intent/translation/context, action cards, and (if applicable) fraud verdict banner.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the provided conversation text into the main conversation input area (the paste textarea).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Hello, thanks for calling. Customer: I\'m very upset—my package never arrived and I need a refund now. Agent: I can help. Can you confirm your order number? Customer: It\'s 12345 and I think someone used my card.')
        
        # -> Click the 'Analyze' button to submit the pasted conversation for analysis and trigger the UI analysis results (transcript, emotion, security, intent, action cards, and fraud banner if applicable).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Transcript').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"transcript-bubbles") or contains(@class,"transcript-bubble")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Emotion').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Security').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"intent") or contains(@id,"intent") or contains(@data-testid,"intent")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Analysis failed banner displayed after clicking the 'Analyze' button, preventing display of analysis results.
- Transcript heading and transcript bubbles are not present on the page after submitting the conversation.
- Emotion gauges / Emotion section are not visible after analysis.
- Security risk indicator / 'Security' section is not visible after analysis.

title: TC009-Transcript bubbles render both speaker turns after analysis completes
description: Verifies the transcript section shows distinct customer and agent turns after running analysis on pasted text.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Input the provided conversation into the main textarea, verify if an Analyze control exists. If no Analyze control exists, report the issue and finish the task.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Agent: Good afternoon. Customer: Your driver left my shipment at the wrong address. Agent: I apologize. Customer: This is the third time and I\'m furious.')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Agent').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Customer').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"transcript") and contains(@class,"bubble")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=wrong address').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()

title: TC010-Emotion/prosody gauges are visible after a successful analysis
description: Verifies the frustration, anxiety, politeness, and confidence gauges appear in the UI after analysis runs.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the provided conversation into the main conversation input area (textarea) so the app can be analyzed; after typing, attempt to locate and click the 'Analyze' button (if present). If 'Analyze' is not present, report the issue and finish the task.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Customer: I\'m anxious because my passport delivery is late. Agent: I understand. Customer: Please confirm it will arrive tomorrow. Agent: I\'ll expedite.')
        
        # -> Click the 'Analyze' button (index 77) to run the analysis and wait for results to render, then verify the four gauges/texts appear.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/div/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Frustration').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Anxiety').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Politeness').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Confidence').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Analysis failed banner displayed after clicking the Analyze button, preventing results from rendering.
- Gauges/texts 'Frustration', 'Anxiety', 'Politeness', and 'Confidence' are not present on the page after analysis attempted.
- The analysis did not complete within the observed wait periods (spinner or error state persisted), so the result UI never rendered.

async def run_test():

            headless=True,

        await expect(frame.locator('text=Security').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
